summary: Small header fix in `src/placeholder.h` changing the `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` macro guard so the MWERKS nop implementation is only selected when compiling with `__MWERKS__` and either `MUST_MATCH` or `WIP` is set. This was intended to fix `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` code generation in objdiff by avoiding an overly broad `#elif MUST_MATCH` branch.

what_changed.change: Changed the preprocessor condition for the MWERKS `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` definition from `#elif MUST_MATCH` to `#elif defined(__MWERKS__) && (MUST_MATCH || WIP)`.
Kept the Clang/GNUC implementation as an `OSPanic(__FILE__, __LINE__, "%s is not implemented!", __func__)` call and the MWERKS matching implementation as `asm { nop }`.

smart_moves: Narrowed the matching-only `asm { nop }` macro to the compiler that can actually consume MWERKS inline assembly, instead of keying only on `MUST_MATCH`.
Included `WIP` alongside `MUST_MATCH`, preserving the lightweight nop placeholder behavior for in-progress matching builds under MWERKS.
Fixed the behavior with a minimal one-line guard change, reducing risk to unrelated placeholder macro behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a macro expands to compiler-specific syntax such as MWERKS inline assembly, guard it with an explicit compiler check like `defined(__MWERKS__)` rather than only a project mode flag.
`MUST_MATCH` and `WIP` describe decompilation/build intent, not compiler capability; combine them with compiler feature checks when selecting codegen-sensitive implementations.
Objdiff-sensitive placeholder macros should avoid generating unsupported code under analysis or non-MWERKS compiler paths.
naming_conventions: `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` is used as a placeholder macro for unimplemented code paths.
`MUST_MATCH` and `WIP` are build/decompilation mode flags used in preprocessor conditionals.
`__MWERKS__`, `__clang__`, and `__GNUC__` are compiler-identification macros used to choose compatible placeholder implementations.
`jmp_t` and `jtbl_t` appear near this macro in `src/placeholder.h`, but the PR did not modify those typedefs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `asm { nop }` for MWERKS matching/WIP placeholder code where a real implementation is absent but codegen shape must remain controlled.
Avoid emitting MWERKS-style inline assembly in Clang/GNUC paths, especially for objdiff workflows.
For non-MWERKS Clang/GNUC builds, `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` remains a diagnostic `OSPanic` call using `__FILE__`, `__LINE__`, and `__func__`.
follow_up_queries: Find other macros using MWERKS inline assembly without `defined(__MWERKS__)` guards.
Check whether other placeholder or WIP macros distinguish compiler capability from decompilation mode flags.
Review objdiff failures involving `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` or placeholder macro expansion.
